repair_candidate.summary: Repair #93897 so the legacy plugins.entries.openai-codex to openai migration preserves legacy-only fields during dual-key configs, reports merge vs rewrite accurately, and warns on true key conflicts instead of silently dropping legacy values.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(doctor): preserve legacy openai-codex plugin entry fields
repair_candidate.pr_body:
## Summary
- Repair the #93897 doctor migration so dual `plugins.entries.openai` and `plugins.entries.openai-codex` configs merge legacy-only fields into the canonical entry.
- Report the dual-key path as a merge instead of a rewrite, and surface true key conflicts as warnings/change messages rather than silently dropping legacy values.
- Add regression coverage for the dual-key merge and conflict paths.

## Credit
This repairs the existing contributor PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/93897 by @lsr911. The issue and review scope came from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/93891 and review notes by @Nas01010101.

## Verification
- `pnpm test src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- Re-run Real behavior proof for #93897 after the branch repair.
credit_notes: Preserve source PR credit for @lsr911 as the #93897 contributor and keep the repair on that maintainer-editable branch.
Preserve issue/review attribution for @Nas01010101 from #93891 and the review comments on #93897.

No replacement PR is planned unless the contributor branch becomes unsafe or uneditable; if replaced later, credit @lsr911 and source PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/93897 in the PR body.
